title: Get Started | Logos
description: Install Basecamp, read the Logos docs, join the community, and start building with the Logos testnet.

sections.heading: Get Started
sections.intro: Everything you need to start building privacy-first, decentralised applications. Install Basecamp, read the docs, learn the stack, write code.

install.heading: Install Basecamp
install.body: Basecamp is the development environment for Logos. It sets up a local node, connects you to the testnet, and provides CLI tools for interacting with all three protocol modules.

docs.title: Read the docs
docs.body: Deep dive into the Logos stack architecture, modules, APIs and integration guides.

scaffold.title: Start with the Scaffold Boilerplate
scaffold.body: A Rust CLI for bootstrapping LEZ (Logos Execution Zone) program_deployment projects in standalone mode.

sampleApps.title: Sample Apps
sampleApps.body: Run real sample apps to learn from.

workshops.title: Workshops and Tutorials
workshops.body: Live coding sessions and step by step guides.

node.title: Run a node
node.body: Participate in the testnet by running a full node. Help validate module integration and stress-test the infrastructure.

messaging.title: Build with Logos Messaging SDK
messaging.body: Add private, censorship-resistant messaging to any application using the JavaScript, Nim, or Go SDKs.

lez.title: Deploy to the LEZ
lez.body: The Logos Execution Zone is the first Sovereign Zone. Deploy applications using the sequencer, block explorer, and core APIs.

storage.title: Store files on Logos Storage
storage.body: Use decentralised storage for application data, documents, or media.
